description: The Gopher Gang was an early 20th-century New York street gang based in Hell's Kitchen, Manhattan. Formed in the 1890s from various local street gangs, it numbered around 500 members and controlled territory from Fourth to Forty-Second Street and Seventh to Eleventh Avenue. The gang engaged in burglary, armed robbery, assault, prostitution, and illegal gambling. It declined in the 1910s.
